sections.heading: This Act may be cited as the Supreme Court of Zambia Title
sections.text:
(Amendment) Act, 2016, and shall be read as one with the Supreme Cap. 25
Court of Zambia Act, in this Act referred to as the principal Act.

sections.heading: Section two of the principal Act is amended by the insertion, Amendment
sections.text:
in the appropriate place, of the following definition: of section 2
“Court of Appeal” means the Court of Appeal established
under Article 130 of the Constitution;. Cap. 1

sections.heading: Section four of the principal Act is amended by the deletion Amendment
sections.text:
of the words “or a final decision in the exercise of its original of section 4
jurisdiction”.

sections.heading: The principal Act is amended by the repeal of section twelve. Repeal of
The principal Act is amended by the repeal of section Repeal of
The principal Act is amended by the repeal of section fifteen Repeal and
sections.text:
and the substitution therefor of the following: replacement
of section 15

sections.heading: (1) The Court shall allow an appeal against Determination
sections.text:
conviction on the following grounds: of appeals
(a) the conviction, in all the circumstances of the case,
is unsafe or unsatisfactory;
Single copies of this Act may be obtained from the Government Printer
P.O. Box 30136, 10101 Lusaka. Price K12.00
572 No. 24 of 2016] Supreme Court (Amendment)
(b) the conviction is based on a wrong decision on a
question of law; or
(c) there was a material irregularity in the course of the
trial.
(2) Despite subsection (1), where the Court is of the
opinion that the point raised in the appeal might be decided in
favour of the appellant, the Court may dismiss the appeal if it
considers that no miscarriage of justice has actually occurred.
(3) The Court shall, if it allows an appeal against
conviction, quash the conviction and direct a judgment and
verdict of acquittal to be entered or, if the interests of justice
so require, order a new trial.
(4) The Court may, on an appeal, whether against
conviction or sentence, substitute a judgment of guilty for
such other offence as the trial court could have entered and,
in the case of an appeal from a judgment of the Court of
Appeal, the Court shall, in addition, have power to restore the
conviction of the trial court.
(5) The Court may, on an appeal, whether against
conviction or sentence, increase or reduce the sentence,
impose such other sentence or make such other order as the
trial court could have imposed or made, except that—
(a) in no case shall a sentence be increased by reason
or in consideration of evidence that was not given
at the trial; and
(b) the Court shall not interfere with a sentence just
because if it were a trial court it would have
imposed a different sentence, unless the sentence
is wrong in principle or comes to the Court with a
sense of shock.
Repeal and 8. The principal Act is amended by the repeal of section
replacement
seventeen and the substitution therefor of the following:
of section 17

sections.heading: (1) A person who intends to appeal against a judgment
sections.text:
Time for
appealing of the Court of Appeal shall give a notice of intention to appeal
within fourteen days of obtaining leave of the Court of Appeal
in the manner and form prescribed by rules of the Court of
Appeal.
Supreme Court (Amendment) [No. 24 of 2016 573
(2) If the intending appellant is in prison, the notice of
intention to appeal or application, as the case may be, may,
within the period of fourteen days referred to in subsection
(1) be given to the officer-in-charge of the prison, who shall
forward it to the Registrar of the Court of Appeal.
(3) The Court may extend the time for giving notice of
intention to appeal or of submitting an application for leave to
appeal despite the time for giving the notice or submitting the
application having already expired.
(4) Where a sentence of death has been passed, an
extension of time referred to in subsection (3) shall not be
granted after the sentence has been confirmed by the
President.

sections.heading: The principal Act is amended by the repeal of section twenty. Repeal of
Section twenty-two of the principal Act is amended— Amendment
sections.text:
of section 22
(a) in subsection (1)—
(i) by the deletion of the words
“High Court” and the substitution therefor of the
words “Court of Appeal”; and
(ii) by the deletion of the words “ three hundred and
thirty-six of the Criminal Procedure Code” and Cap. 88
the substitution therefor of the words “ eighteen
of the Court of Appeal Act, 2016”; and Act No. 7 of
2016
(b) by the repeal of subsection (2) and the substitution therefor
of the following:
(2) The time during which an appellant, pending
the determination of the appellant’s appeal—
(a) is admitted to bail and, subject to any
directions which the Court may give to
the contrary in an appeal; or
(b) if in custody, is treated as an unconvicted
prisoner under this section;
shall not count as part of any term of imprisonment
under the appellant’s sentence.
574 No. 24 of 2016] Supreme Court (Amendment)
(3) The imprisonment of an appellant referred
to in subsection (2) shall, subject to any directions
which the Court may give to the contrary, be deemed to
be resumed or to begin to run, as the case requires —
(a) if the appellant is in custody, from the
day on which the appeal is determined;
or
(b) if the appellant is not in custody, from
the day on which the appellant is
received into jail under the sentence.
Amendment 11. Part IV of the principal Act is amended, in the heading, by
of Part IV the deletion of the words “HIGH COURT” and the substitution
therefor of the words “COURT OF APPEAL”.

sections.heading: The principal Act is amended by the repeal of section
sections.text:
Repeal and
replacement twenty-three and the substitution therefor of the following:
of
section 23
Right of 23. Subject to the exceptions and restrictions contained
appeal in in section twenty-four, an appeal in a civil case or matter
civil cases
shall lie to the Court from a judgment of the Court of Appeal.
Amendment 13. The principal Act is amended by the deletion of section
of section 24 twenty-four and the substitution therefor of the following:
Restrictions 24. An appeal shall not lie—
on civil
(a) from an order allowing an extension of time for
appeals
appealing from a judgment;
(b) from a judgment given by the Court of Appeal without
the leave of that court or, if that has been refused,
without the leave of a judge of the Court;
(d) from an order of the Court of Appeal or any judge
of the Court of Appeal made with the consent of
the parties or from an order relating to costs only
which by law is left to the discretion of the court
without the leave of the court or of the judge who
made the order or, if that has been refused, without
the leave of a judge of the Court;
(e) from an order made in chambers by a judge of the
Court of Appeal or from an interlocutory order or
interlocutory judgment made or given by a judge
of the Court of Appeal, without the leave of the
judge or, if that has been refused, without the leave
of a judge of the Court.
Supreme Court (Amendment) [No. 24 of 2016 575
